channel.title: Condensed Matter🍩 + AI🤖 Journal Club🎓
channel.description: Lecture notes from the condensed matter theory journal club organized by Yiyang Jiang at Penn State.

item.title: AI Workflow & Vibe Researching
item.author: yzj5306@psu.edu (Yiyang Jiang)
item.description:
Presenter Yiyang Jiang
Date July 6, 2026
Topic AI Workflow & Vibe Researching

The presentation argues that for modern physics research, the key question is no longer whether to use AI, but how to use it effectively. It distinguishes two major roles: NN-based AI for principles (neural-network fitting, neural quantum states, DeepH) and LLM-based AI for workflow (planning, memory, tools, APIs, MCP, agent loops).

item.title: Topological Insulators and the Bulk–Boundary Correspondence
item.author: yzj5306@psu.edu (Yiyang Jiang)
item.description:
Presenter Yiyang Jiang
Date June 30, 2026
Venue Topology Seminar
Notes Typeset & handwritten versions below

These notes build the bulk–boundary correspondence of topological band insulators from the ground up — from the modern theory of polarization to the protected edge spectrum and the wider topological zoo. Both the typeset write-up and the original handwritten notes are attached below.

item.title: Experimental Realization of Topological Insulator
item.author: yzj5306@psu.edu (Yiyang Jiang)
item.description:
Presenter Pu Xiao
Date June 23, 2026
Topic Experimental Realization of Topological Insulator
Notes Slides & PDF below

A survey of how topological insulators are actually made and measured: from the band-inversion picture and the 2D/3D material platforms that realize it, to the experimental probes — ARPES, molecular beam epitaxy, and STM/STS — that reveal and characterize the protected surface states.
